https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: PER_ABSENCE_ATTENDANCES
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: This table holds details of employee absences from work. Records can be entered with PROJECTED dates and later confirmed as actual. Projected absences have no impact on absence balances.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: PER_ABSENCE_ATTENDANCE_TYPES_B
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description:
PER_ABSENCE_ATTENDANCE_TYPES holds user definitions of absence types.
These types are used to identify specific types of absence or
attendance in PER_ABSENCE_ATTENDANCES. You can associate an absence
type with the input value of a non-recurring element to maintain a
running total of time taken. When an absence of this type is
subsequently given to an employee, a nonrecurring element entry is
automatically created for his or her primary assignment using the
duration of HOURS_OR_DAYS. The INCREASING_OR_DESCENDING_FLAG
determines whether the absence duration is mutiplied by -1 and the
running total for an assignment is the sum of all entries of
INPUT_VALUE_ID on the primary assignment.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: PER_ABSENCE_CASES
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: This table is to store the Details of Absence Cases. Absence Case is a collection of Absences of an Employee grouped manually based on some criteria.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: PER_ABS_ATTENDANCE_REASONS
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description:
PER_ABS_ATTENDANCE_REASONS holds the list of absence reasons that may
apply to an absence or attendance type. Reasons are selected from the
list of values defined for the lookup ABSENCE_REASON.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: PER_ABS_ATTENDANCE_TYPES_TL
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: PER_ABS_ATTENDANCE_TYPES_TL holds translated information for Absence Types.

This stores two levels of the 3-Tier Model: Employment/Placement Terms (Level 2) and Assignments (Level 3). The assignment type is used to differentiate between these two levels as well as it continues to differentiate among employee, contingent worker, applicants, and benefits assignments.

This table stores all information related to the person?s Work Relationships. An Work Relationship denotes a formal relationship between a person and a legal employer.  Therefore, this table will hold information for employees and contingent workers.

Current design allows for a person to have multiple active ?periods of service? as long as they are for different legal entities. This is not Effective Dated.

For enforced balances this cannot be overridden. Otherwise, it acts as a default specification of which elements feed a balance.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: This table contains the association between a card, or a section of a card, and a tax reporting unit, thus forming a reporting card.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: This table contains mappings of deduction components to employment terms or assignments within a reporting card.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: PAY_ELEMENT_CRITERIA
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: This table contains combinations of eligibility criteria that are referenced by element eligibilty (PAY_ELEMENT_LINKS_F) and payroll relationship, assigned payroll, and payroll assignment records. There is one record for each unique combination (for example a specific PAYROLL_ID and JOB_ID).

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: PAY_ELEMENT_ENTRIES_F
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: This table contains element entries for each assignment. The actual values for each entry are held in PAY_ELEMENT_ENTRY_VALUES_F. An entry represents a specific instance of an element for a particular assignment. For example, there may be a salary element and each assignment receives a salary element entry.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: PAY_ELEMENT_ENTRIES_F_
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: This table contains element entries for each assignment. The actual values for each entry are held in PAY_ELEMENT_ENTRY_VALUES_F. An entry represents a specific instance of an element for a particular assignment. For example, there may be a salary element and each assignment receives a salary element entry.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: PAY_ELEMENT_ENTRY_VALUES_F
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: This table contains the actual values entered for a specific element entry. For example, the overtime element may have an input value of hours worked. The number of hours worked by an employee in a fixed period is the entry value for that period.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: PAY_ELEMENT_ENTRY_VALUES_F_
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: This table contains the actual values entered for a specific element entry. For example, the overtime element may have an input value of hours worked. The number of hours worked by an employee in a fixed period is the entry value for that period.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: PAY_ELEMENT_LINKS_F
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: This table contains the eligibility rules that link elements to groups of employees. An assignment must match the eligibility criteria defined for the element link before it can have an entry of that element. Default values for the element input values can be set or overridden for each link. The criteria values themselves are not held on the element link entity directly. Instead, these are held on a criteria combination table (PAY_ELEMENT_CRITERIA).

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: PAY_ELEMENT_SPAN_USAGES
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: This table contains information to determine which retropay element to use depending on the age of the retrospective change. For example, if a correction is processed from the previous tax year, there may be legislative rules concerning different taxation that needs to be applied.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: PAY_ELEMENT_TYPES_F
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: This table contains the definitions of elements, which are the units used to build all earnings and benefits. NOTE: Users must not enter information into the Developer Descriptive Flexfield columns of this table. These are reserved for the use of localization and verticalization teams, for entry and maintenance of legislative or industry-specific data.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: PAY_ELEMENT_TYPES_TL
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: This table contains translation information for PAY_ELEMENT_TYPES_F.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: PAY_ELEMENT_TYPE_USAGES_F
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: Used to identify how  the Element is used with the Run Type

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: PAY_ELE_CLASSIFICATIONS
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: This table contains element classifications that define groups of elements for legislative and informational needs. Primary classifications are specific to a legislation and are predefined for all supported payroll legislations. Secondary or sub-classifications are specific to a legislative group and may be predefined for a specific legislation. Secondary classifications can be associated with an element to manage taxability rules.All types of classifications have a role in creating balance feeds automatically when balances and elements are created.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: PAY_ELE_CLASSIFICATIONS_TL
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: This table contains translation information for PAY_ELE_CLASSIFICATIONS.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: Table For Element Class Usages PAY_ELE_CLASS_USAGES_F

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: This table contains the periods in which an element should be processed. For example, if a deduction should process in periods 2 and 3 of a month for employees on a weekly payroll, it would record periods 2 and 3 and ensure 1 and 4 are not processed.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: This table contains a percentage break down of an entry value across one or more payroll terms or assignments. These percentages are used for costing. The values have two decimal places of precision and must total 100 exactly across all the rows for the element entry.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: This table is useful for creating transfer chunks in the data base.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: HRC_DL_UI_BUS_OBJECTS_B
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: HRC_DL_BUS_OBJECTS_B is seeded as a base Standard MLS table

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: HRC_DL_UI_BUS_OBJECTS_TL
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: HRC_DL_BUS_OBJECTS_TL is seeded as a language dependent Standard MLS table to provide localized business object names and descriptions for UI display.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: HRC_DL_VO_ATTR_MAPS
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: HRC_DL_VO_ATTR_MAPS is used to record mappings used for individual View Object attributes at import time so that they may be referenced at load time for the staged physical data row being processed.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: HRC_DL_VO_FLEX_MAPS
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: This table is used to store flex information for VO maps

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: HRC_DL_VO_MAPS
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description:
HRC_DL_VO_MAPS is used to record the View Objects mapped at import time so that they may be referenced at load time to ensure the appropriate VO data is located from the Data Loader stage table columns.
When data is imported for a business object, Metadata manager analyses the ADF View Object to determine the names and types of Data Loader eligible attributes used in the View Object.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: HRC_DL_VO_TABLES
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: HRC_DL_VO_TABLES is used to record the Table Name mapped to the Business Object VO and the annotations defined in the VO. These tables will be used later to perform the gather statistics during HDL Load process.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: HRC_DL_VO_USER_KEY_ATTRS
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: HRC_DL_VO_USER_KEY_ATTRS is used to record information for the user keys that correspond to any surrogate keys that have been mapped for a View Object.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: HRC_DL_VO_USER_KEY_SQL
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description:
This table is used to store the additional import query for each and every object.

Data will not be available for following conditions :

1) transient objects
2) translation objects

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: HRC_EVT_ATTRIBUTE_ARCHIVE_REQS
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description:
An Archive Attribute identifies that a particular entity object attribute or PLSQL row handler column should be stored in the Source Object Change tables. There is the option to always store the column/attribute value regardless of whether the value changed or to only store it when its value changes. In addition it is possible to indicate whether both Old and New values are required.
N.B. If no archive attribute records are created, then a detected event will still be stored in the Source Object Change tables, however there will be no attribute data stored The data that will be stored in this case will be the primary key data and, in the case of date effective change, the date ranges over which the changes took place.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: HRC_EVT_ATTRIBUTE_MAPPINGS
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: A Attribute Mapping is used to indicate that a particular Entity Object or PLSQL Row Handler attribute should be included in an Event Payload.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: HRC_EVT_CONDITIONS
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description:
An Event Condition provides qualifying criteria for an event type and is used to refine the circumstances under which the event will be detected. Depending on the event type??????s object, the types of condition will vary, for instance the Attribute condition is only relevant for EO and PLSQL Row Handler criteria whilst an Action Reason is only relevant for Actions.
If an event type has multiple event conditions they will be evaluated together using an OR clause to merge them e.g.  PersonEO event detected if FIRST_NAME or LAST_NAME changed

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: HRC_EVT_CONSTRUCTION_METHODS
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description:
A Construction Method defines how a published event will be raised in terms of the technology that will be used. It specifies whether the payload will be constructed from a set of defined attributes (in which case these attributes are listed in the Attribute Mappings table), whether it will be derived using a Java method or a PLSQL procedure, or whether it will be derived from attributes passed into a service.

If the Java method is used then a custom Java class must be defined which should contain the method named "constructPayload".

If "Service" is chosen, this indicates that a standard HCM Event service will have been raised to invoke the HCM Events model. As part of the service a set of generic service parameters will have been populated which are subsequently mapped to Event Payload Items.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: HRC_EVT_CONTEXT_DERIVATIONS
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: A context derivation method identifies the mechanism that will be used to derive a context value when processing a particular event object. The context can either be derived from a named EO attribute, including transient attributes, a PLSQL row handler parameter or by using a Java method which is based on a business process root application module.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: HRC_EVT_CONTEXT_RULES
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description:
A Context Rule is used to qualify a Subscription Set and defines the context value(s) that will cause the Subscription Set's event handlers to be processed. It is possible to define a context value as either Included or Excluded; if the value is included then if the Source Object's Context value matches the Context Rule the Event handler will be processed, whilst if the value is Excluded, then the Event handler will be processed as long as the event object's context does not match the excluded value.

When there are multiple context rules for a subscription set the Included values are combined using an OR clause and the Excluded values are combined using an AND clause
e.g. (Context = 'A' OR Context = 'B') AND (Context <> 'X' AND Context <> 'Y')

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: HRC_EVT_DEFINITIONS
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: An Event Definition is used to register the deployed event definitions with the HCM Events model, so that it can be used to programmatically build up the definition of the event on detection of a relevant change within the HCM system.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: HRC_EVT_HANDLERS
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description:
An Event Handler identifies how a particular event will be managed once it is determined that its criteria has been satisfied.

Detection of an event can result in
a) an event being published to the SOA server (payload is dependent on the type of event)
b) a standard event (i.e. the payload is a fixed shape) is published to the SOA server; this can represent either a data change, in which case the standard event will contain the Source Object Change ID, or a transaction framework based event, in which case the payload will contain the Transaction ID
c) an internal call to a synchronous process, allowing invocation of processing in the same transaction
d) storage of data in the Source Object Change tables without publishing an event to SOA.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: HRC_EVT_OBJ_CHANGES
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description:
A Source Object Change Instancet identifies a particular entity object or PLSQL row handler record that changed as part of a transaction. The PK1 - PK9 values correspond to the Source Object Attribute record PRIMARY_KEY and PK_SEQ columns.
N.B. The Effective Dates of a date effective EO are not stored in this table, only the primary key of the logical DE entity.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: HRC_EVT_OBJ_CHANGE_COMPONENTS
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: An Source Object Change Component stores information about the date range over which a specific change takes place. In the case of a non-DE change there will be a single record with no Validation start/end dates. In the case of a DE change each individual change that is part of a series of changes brought about by the DE Operation will be captured.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: HRC_EVT_OBJ_CHG_ATTRIBUTES
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: A Source Object Change Attribute stores the old and new  values of a particular EO or Row Handler attribute when that attribute changes within a transaction.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: HRC_EVT_OBJ_CONDITIONS_B
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: An Event Condition provides qualifying criteria for an object and is used to refine the circumstances under which the event will be detected.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: HRC_EVT_OBJ_CONDITIONS_TL
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: Contains translatable information about Event Conditions.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: HRC_EVT_OBJ_COND_USAGES
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: An Event Condition provides qualifying criteria for an object and is used to refine the circumstances under which the event will be detected.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: HRC_EVT_OBJ_HANDLER_USAGES
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: Contains mapping of handlers and conditions for the given reference type and id

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: HRC_EVT_PAYLOAD_ITEMS
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: A Payload Item defines an instance of an XML TAG attribute in an Event Definition.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: HRC_EVT_SOURCE_OBJECTS
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: A Source Object contains the details of object from which events can be raised and includes Entity Objects, Business Processes, HR Actions and PLSQL Row Handlers

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: HRC_EVT_SRC_OBJ_ATTRIBUTES
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: An source object attribute is an attribute or column that exists within an Entity Object or a PLSQL row Handle

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: HRC_EVT_SUBSCRIPTION_LINES
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: A Subscription Line represents the inclusion of an Event Type in a Subscription Set.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: HRC_EVT_SUBSCRIPTION_SETS
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description:
A Subscription Set groups a set of Internal Event handlers and associates them with one or more context rules for the purposes of determining whether the Event handler should actually be processed.
If an event handler is in a subscription set then the context rule must be satisfied before the event handler is processed.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: HRC_EVT_TYPES
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: An Event Type identifies a significant change on a data object or a particular trigger point within a process flow. It can be further qualified by Event Conditions and is used to detect the points at which subsequent processing should be invoked.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: HRC_INTEGRATION_KEY_MAP
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: HRC_INTEGRATION_KEY_MAP stores the  Integration Key Map definitions.  SurrogateId and SourceSystem id Key-Mapping is stored in this table

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: HRC_INTEGRATION_OBJECTS
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: HRC_INTEGRATION_OBJECTS stores the Object definitions. Stores ObjectName alsong with underlying BaseTableName

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: HRC_LOADER_JOB

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: HRC_MESSAGE_CATEGORIES_B

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: HRC_MESSAGE_CONFIG_TL
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: This table is required for storing the transalated data for message configuration.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: HRC_MESSAGE_DELIVERY
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: Table to track the messages delivery

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: HRC_MESSAGE_HISTORY

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: HRC_MESSAGE_USAGE_IDN

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: HRC_TXN_CONDITIONS_CONTEXT
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: This table contains information to identify a particular usage of Condition Builder. When product teams uptake Condition Builder feature, they create entries in this table to denote that they have up-taken Condition Builder and have metadata associated with that particular uptake. This table has two main columns, CONDITIONS_CONTEXT_MODULE and CONTEXT_PROCESS_NAME which identifies the usage of the Condition Builder component. CONDITIONS_CONTEXT_MODULE contains information about the UI (like Approvals UI) and CONDITIONS_PROCESS_NAME further contains a sub-process in that UI, if applicable, which provides more information about where the Condition Builder is used. As an eg, for Approvals UI, CONDITIONS_CONTEXT_MODULE would contain the code for Approvals UI and the CONDITIONS_PROCESS_NAME would contain the Human task name. These two fields together would provide the exact context information to the Condition Builder Task Flow.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: HRC_TXN_CONDITIONS_LOVMETADATA
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: HRC_TXN_CONDITIONS_LOVMETADATA contains all required information to generate a SQL query from a particular View Object, with a provision for applying View Criterias and Bind variables to it. This generated query drives the LOV that is displayed in the Condition Builder component. The product teams would provide the View Object name from where we can extract the query. Further they can provide View Criterias and Bind variable details to be applied on the View Object before the query is generated. Finally they provide names of two columns of the View Object's query that are displayed in the UI's LOV field. Please refer to individual columns for more informaiton.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: HRC_TXN_CONDITIONS_LOVUSAGE
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: HRC_TXN_CONDITIONS_LOVUSAGE is a mapping table which maps a participar attribute, identified by the CONDITION_OPERAND column of this table, to its metadata information, identified by the reference to the HRC_TXN_CONDITIONS_LOVMETADATA table, in a particual UI context, which is identified by a reference to HRC_TXN_CONDITIONS_CONTEXT. So essentially the Condition Builder component can generate an LOV query from the metadata table(referred through LOV_METADATA_ID) for an attribute that is present in a particular context (referred through CONDITIONS_CONTEXT_ID).

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description:
This table is used to capture profile interests of employee. 
Interest lists enable employees to maintain a list of the non-person profiles that are of interest. The interest list can include any profile. 
For example, employees could add profiles for jobs that they want to include in their career planning, or job profiles that are similar to their own profile. Each employee has one interest list that can be modified by the employee, the employee's manager, and the profile administrator

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description:
This table stores the Profile Items of the Source Profile that form the Match Criteria in the Profile Best Fit.

This table will act as a kind of temporary table storing the Matching Criteria so that the final query to match the Source Profile with the Target profiles is efficient and simpler. This table will have a structure similar to the HRT_PROFILE_ITEMS table with certain additional columns needed for Match.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: IRC_BOT_QUESTIONS_CONFIG_B
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description:
The irc_bot_questions_config_b along with the irc_bot_questions_config_tl table (translated) store the predefined questions and answers that an Admin configures for candidates to be presented with when the candidates want to know more about a requisition. This table stores the metadata information for a particular pre-defined question on a requisition-- example QUESTION_CATEGORY.

Three question categories are available, questions on Standard fields, questions on Flex fields and general questions that are not any tied to any field.

Questions can be enabled/ disabled and the sequence for the questions can be defined within these settings.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: IRC_BOT_QUESTIONS_CONFIG_TL
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description:
The irc_bot_questions_config_b along with the irc_bot_questions_config_tl table (translated) store the predefined questions and answers that an Admin configures for candidates to be presented with when the candidates want to know more about a requisition. This table stores the translated questions and answers in the different languages that the Recruiting Admin has defined.

Three question categories are available, questions on Standard fields, questions on Flex fields and general questions that are not any tied to any field.

Questions can be enabled/ disabled and the sequence for the questions can be defined within these settings.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description:
This table is used to maintain Geography level mappings for each country . This is a 3 level mapping table where for each country a mapping entry is defined to refer a colum name in HZ_GEOGRAPHIES table for level2 and level3 values.

The main purpose if having a 3 level geo mapping table is while location indexing, the FQN gets built only on 3 levels like "City, State,Country".

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: HXT_TM_MTRX_DLY_ATRS

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: HXT_TM_MTRX_DLY_ATR_LNKS

We will link this table with the  HXT_TM_MTRX table via the TM_MTRX_ROW_ID and to denote which detail entry this additional detail belongs to via the DETAIL_ENTRY_DAY Column.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: HXT_TM_MTRX_DTL_ATRS
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: This table is designed to store the reverse orientation of data stored in HXT_TM_MTRX_DLY_ATRS to allow configuration on the two types of details dialog layout.  The extra copy of data exists for performance reasons.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: HWM_TCSMR_CONFIGS
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: Each Time Consumer Configuration Set has a row in this table for each of the Time Consumers that is a member of the set. A row in this table details the configuration information for that Time Consumer, such as the Time Category to be used for deciding to process a Time Entry

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: HWM_TCSMR_CONFIG_SETS_B
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: A consumer configuration set represents a group of time consumers registering interest in time.  This is used in a setup option such that the time entered by users is identified as required by consumers specified in this set.  Futher attribution is kept on the set to identify when consumers should receive time, when consumer validation should execute and under what conditions time should be transferred to the consumer.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: HWM_TCSMR_CONFIG_SETS_TL
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: A consumer configuration set represents a group of time consumers registering interest in time.  This is used in a setup option such that the time entered by users is identified as required by consumers specified in this set.  Futher attribution is kept on the set to identify when consumers should receive time, when consumer validation should execute and under what conditions time should be transferred to the consumer.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: HWM_TCSMR_SETS
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: Contains the header record for a Time Consumer Set. In Fusion 1.1 there is no UI for the creation of time consumer sets

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: HWM_TCSMR_XFR_CONFIGS
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: For each Time Consumer in the Time Consumer Configuration Set there is typically a transfer process that needs to be configured. The data model allows for more than a single transfer process for each Time Consumer. This table contains a set of rows (where a set contains a row for each Time Consumer in the Time Consumer Configuration Set) for each transfer process linked to a given time consumer configuration

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_ACRS_PTIP_CVG_F
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_ACRS_PTIP_CVG_F holds the maximum coverage or benefit value for multiple plan types in the same program.  For example, if you have plan types of Basic Employee Life Insurance and Supplemental Life Insurance, you could define here that if a person enrolls in  plans of both of these plan types,  the total coverage a person is allowed to elect is 1,000,000.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_ACTN_TYP
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_ACTN_TYP is an action required to complete enrollment, either for enrollment results or enrollment events.  It includes Requirement to Designate Beneficiary, Designate  Dependent Provide Proof of Good Health,  Obtain Third Party Administrator Approval.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_ACTN_TYP_TL

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_ACTY_BASE_RT_CTFN_F
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_ACTY_BASE_RT_CTFN_F identifies the certifications that may be required to receive a particular rate.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_ACTY_BASE_RT_F
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_ACTY_BASE_RT_F identifies parameters which control how contributions into, distributions out of, or accrued benefits provided by comp objects are calculated.  Activity Rates are maintained at many  levels within the comp hierarchy, for example, program, plan, option in plan, plan type in program.   . Examples are employee payroll contributions in  the amount of $25.43 per month for a coverage option in a medical plan, employer payroll contributions in  the amount of $35.77 per month based on participant's age of 25 , for each thousand dollars of life insurance coverage, and employer payroll distribution of $50.00 per month for flex credits.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_ACTY_RT_DED_SCHED_F
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_ACTY_RT_DED_SCHED_F  identifies the deduction schedule(s) to used  to control how frequently a contribution-related activity base rate is to be deducted. BEN_ACTY_RT_DED_SCHED_F is the intersection of BEN_ACTY_BASE_RT_F  and BEN_DED_SCHED_PY_FREQ.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_ACTY_RT_PTD_LMT_F
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_ACTY_RT_PTD_LMT_F identifies what period to date limits apply to an activity base rate.  Example, the employee payroll deduction for a specific savings plan is subjected to a maximum  year to date limit which restricts the maximum contributions allowed for this and other such plans. BEN_ACTY_RT_PTD_LMT_F is the intersection of BEN_ACTY_BASE_RT_F  and BEN_PTD_LMT_F.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_ACTY_RT_PYMT_SCHED_F
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_ACTY_RT_PYMT_SCHED_F identifies the payment schedule(s) to use to control how frequently a distribution-related activity base rate is to be paid.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_ACTY_VRBL_RT_F
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description:
BEN_ACTY_VRBL_RT_F identifies the variable rate profiles which prescribe how to calculate activity base rates , contributions, distributions, and accrued benefits whose calculation varies by some factor,for example, length of service, age, location.
BEN_ACTY_VRBL_RT_F is the intersection of BEN_ACTY_BASE_RT_F and  BEN_VRBL_RT_F.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_AGE_FCTR
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_AGE_FCTR  identifies how to calculate a person's age for such purposes as determining eligibility and calculating rates, in those cases when  age is a factor.  It may also identify a range of ages, again for  eligibility and rate purposes.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_APLD_DPNT_CVG_ELIG_PRFL_F
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_APLD_DPNT_CVG_ELIG_PRFL_F specifieswhich dependent coverage eligiblity profiles are being used by which programs, plan types in programs, plans in programs, or plans.  Although many  dependent coverage eligibility profiles may be specified,  the potentially covered dependent must satisfy all required profiles and one of any optional profiles.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_BATCH_ACTN_ITEM_INFO
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_BATCH_ITEM_INFO  contains action item related data resulting from running concurrent manager processes. . BEN_BATCH tables store concurrent manager results so that this information is available for inclusion in reports, such as the activity summary report and audit log. This information is stored regardless of whether the process is run in validate (rollback) or commit mode.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_BATCH_LINES

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_BATCH_PARAMETER
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_BATCH_PARAMETER identifies the number of threads, the chunk size per thread, and maximum  number of errors allowed per thread before that thread is errored,  for each benefits concurrent  manager process and business group.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_BATCH_PROC_INFO
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_BATCH_PROC_INFO  contains statistical data about each benefits concurrent manager process that was run.  It includes information such as the start and end time of the job, elapsed time, number of persons processed, and the number of errors generated.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_BATCH_RANGES
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_BATCH_RANGES contains ranges of people that are going to be processedby multi-threaded programs. The number of people in a range is set by the chunk size for that particular concurrent program. The threads within a concurrent program attempt to process ranges of people until all ranges have been exhausted. The total number of ranges will be the number of people to be processed divided by the chunk size.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_BATCH_RATE_INFO
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_BATCH_RATE_INFO contains rate related data resulting from running concurrent manager processes. . BEN_BATCH tables store concurrent manager results so that this information is available for inclusion in reports, such as the activity summary report and audit log. This information is stored regardless of whether the process is run in validate (rollback) or commit mode.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_BENEFIT_ACTIONS
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_BENEFIT_ACTIONS  contains the run parameters for the benefits concurrent manager processing, such as the Participation Process and Extract Process, run by the concurrent manager.  This information is used mainly to restart the batch process.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_BENEFIT_QSTNR

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_BENEFIT_RELATIONS_F

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_BENFTS_GRP
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_BENFTS_GRP identifies a name and description that may be assigned to one or more persons who share common benefits processing characteristics, typically in eligibility or in the variable rates assigned to them.   Using a benefits group can simplify tasks such as determining who is eligible to participate in a plan, or applying contribution rates to a  plan.  . For example, Vision Corporation defines Benefits Group 10 to categorize employees with lengths of service greater than five years in a particular subsidiary that Vision Corporation recently acquired.  Only these employees are eligible for the Company Car plan that Vision has discontinued for the rest of its employees. In another example, Vision Corporation defines Benefits Group 1 to categorize employees who are 61 to 65 years of age. A special contribution rate for Vision Corporation's self-insured Live Long Life Insurance plan applies to Benefits Group.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_BILL_PAYMENTS contains information related to Benefits Billing Payments and Captures each payment made by person with necessary details.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_BILL_PAYMENTS contains information related to Benefits Billing Payments and Captures each payment made by person with necessary details.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_BILL_PER_CREDIT
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: This table stores amounts from conversion or credits or adjustments

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_BNFTS_BAL_F
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_BENFTS_BAL_F  identifies a name and unit of measure for balance type  information which is not readily derivable from information contained in  the system or which may be provided by a third party agent, such as an outside payroll, timekeeping system, participant recordkeeper or an actuary.   For example, hours worked or compensation history. . You may associate a benefits balance with a person and then record  the value for that person.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_BNFT_CATEGORY
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description:
Benefit Category..
Benefit Category..
Benefit Category..
Benefit Category..
Benefit Category..

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_BNFT_CATEGORY_TL
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_BNFT_CATEGORY_TL holds MLS translated data from main table, BEN_BNFT_CATEGORY.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_BNFT_RSTRN_CTFN_F
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_BNFT_RSTRN_CTFN_F identifies the certification(s) that may be required when a restriction has been placed on the levels of benefits or coverage, such as life insurance.  . For example, the plan provides coverage from 10,000 to 100,000  in increments of 10,000.  The person may only increase his or her coverage up  to two multiples of 10,000 and certification must be provided if he or she elects  higher coverage.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_BNFT_VRBL_RT_F
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_BNFT_VRBL_RT_F specifies the variable rate profiles used to calculate a rate for a benefit. . For example,  life insurance coverage benefits may be calculated based on whether the person uses tobacco.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_BNFT_VRBL_RT_RL_F
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_BNFT_VRBL_RT_RL_F  identifies the Fast Formula(e)  to be used to calculate a rate for a benefit. . For example, life insurance coverage benefits may be calculated based on a formula which examines information in the person flexfields or other information or combinations of information not available in delivered rate criteria.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_CIR_MAPPINGS_F
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description:
BEN_CIR_MAPPINGS_F identifies which Payroll Component is associated to which
Plan or Option in Plan

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_CLPSE_LF_EVT
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_CLPSE_LF_EVT identifies the life event(s) that will be consolidated into one resulting life even when being evaluated concurrently. It also specifies the number of days apart from each other the group of one or more life events must have occurred, the rule or formula of collapsing and status the collapsed life events should be set to. For example,VOID.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_CLPSE_LF_EVT_F
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_CLPSE_LF_EVT_F  identifies the life event(s) that will be consolidated into one  resulting life even when being evaluated concurrently.  It also specifies the number of days apart from each other the group of one or more life events must have occurred, and status the  collapsed life events should be set to.  For example,Voided.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_CMBN_AGE_LOS_FCTR
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_CMBN_AGE_LOS_FCTR  identifies how to calculate a person's combined age and length of service for such  purposes as determining eligibility and calculating rates, in those  cases when combined age and length of service is a factor, such  as reductions in force where a person qualifies for severence  benefits if he or she has a combination of 75 points,for example,. is 50 years  old and has 25 years of service.  In addition it identifies a range  of combined ages and lengths-of-service,  again for eligibility and  rate purposes.  It is an intersection of BEN_AGE_FCTR  and BEN_LOS_FCTR.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_CMBN_PLIP_F
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_CMBN_PLIP_F  is a collection of plans in a program that may provide credits to a participant for enrolling in one or more  plans in the program.  For example, the combination of Vision LTD Plan, Vision STD, and Vision Sick Time may be combined to provide  credits if a participant enrolls in any one of the plans.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_CMBN_PTIP_F
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_CMBN_PTIP_F  is a collection of plan types ina program that may provide credits to a participant for enrolling in one or more plans of the specified plan types in the program. For example, the combination plan type in program of "Health Benefit" may be  comprised of Medical, Dental, and Vision Plan Types.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_CMBN_PTIP_OPT_F
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_CMBN_PTIP_OPT_F  is a collection of plan types in a program further refined by option that may provide credits to a participant for enrolling in one or more plans of the specified plan types and options in the program. . For example, the combination plan type in program of "Health Benefit Participant Only" may be comprised of Medical Participant Only, Dental Participant Only, and  Vision Participant Only Plan Types and Options.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_CNTNG_PRTN_ELIG_PRFL
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_CNTNG_PRTN_ELIG_PRFL_F identifies the eligibility criteria for participation in a compensation plan on a continuing basis. For example,  after a life event occurs which typically disqualifies a participant  from participation and/or reduces/eliminates any employer contribution subsidy.   . In the US, continuing participation is a focus for COBRA, in and outside the US, plan sponsors may allow prior participants in a  plan (typically medical) to continue to participate if that person pays the premiums.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_CNTNU_PRTN_CTFN_TYP_F
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_CNTNU_PRTN_CTFN_TYP_F identifies the certification that may be required in order to continue participating in a plan.  The certification is associated with a continuing participation eligibility  profile, which  identifies the eligibility criteria for participation in a compensation plan on a continuing basis. For example,  after a life event occurs which typically disqualifies a participant from participation and/or reduces/eliminates any employer contribution subsidy. . In the US, continuing participation is a focus for COBRA, in and outside the US, plan sponsors may allow prior participants in a  plan (typically medical) to continue to participate if that person pays  the premiums.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_COMP_LVL_FCTR
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_COMP_LVL_FCTR  identifies how to calculate a person's compensation level,for example, base pay, for such purposes as determining eligibility and calculating rates, in those cases when compensation level is a factor. In addition it identifies a range of compensation  levels, again for eligibility and rate purposes.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_COMP_OBJ_CACHE
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_COMP_OBJECT_CACHE holds the compensation objects cache for a run of the manage life events process.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_COMP_OBJ_CACHE_ROW
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_COMP_OBJECT_CACHE_ROW holds the denormalized compensation information for a run of the Manage life events process.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_CRDT_POOL_SPND_RLVR_OPT_F
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_CRDT_POOL_SPND_RLVR_OPT_F stores the activity base rates which are specified as spending or rollover options for credit pools defined for flex programs.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_CRT_ORDR
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_CRT_ORDR  identifies orders issued by legislative bodies for a person and a plan or  a plan type.  A person may have multiple orders for the same plan covering different  related persons.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_CRT_ORDR_CVRD_PER
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_CRT_ORDR_CVRD_PER  identifies the persons who are the beneficiaries of  orders issued by legislative bodies for a person and a plan or a plan type.   For example, if a person is required by a legislative action to designate his or her child under  a medical plan, the child is the court order covered person.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_CRT_ORDR_F
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_CRT_ORDR_F identifies orders issued by legislative bodies for a person and a plan or a plan type. A person may have multiple orders for the same plan covering different related persons.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_CRT_ORDR_F_
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_CRT_ORDR_F identifies orders issued by legislative bodies for a person and a plan or a plan type. A person may have multiple orders for the same plan covering different related persons.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_CRT_ORDR_PL_DPNT
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description:
BEN_CRT_ORDR_PL_DPNT identifies the persons who are the beneficiaries of 
orders issued by legislative bodies for a person and a plan or a plan type. 
For example, if a person is required by a legislative action to designate his or her child under a medical plan, the child is the court order covered person.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_CRT_ORDR_PL_DPNT_
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description:
BEN_CRT_ORDR_PL_DPNT identifies the persons who are the beneficiaries of 
orders issued by legislative bodies for a person and a plan or a plan type. 
For example, if a person is required by a legislative action to designate his or her child under a medical plan, the child is the court order covered person.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_CSR_ACTIVITIES
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_CSR_ACTIVITIES holds the list of forms and online processes, that are to be navigable from the Oracle Advanced Benefits, Benefits Service Center form. For example, process life events.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_CSS_RLTD_PER_PER_IN_LER
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_CSS_RLTD_PER_PER_IN_LER  identifies which life event reported by or detected for a participant may spawn a life event for other persons related to him or her.   For example, a triggered life event of death for a participant causes a loss of coverage life event to related persons.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_CTFN_DOC_MAP
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_CTFN_DOC_MAP is a mapping table between certification type and Document Type. This mapping is allowed at two levels(Program or PNIP)

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_CVG_AMT_CALC_MTHD_F
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_CVG_AMT_CALC_MTHD_F  identifies what the coverage amount is or how coverage is calculated for a plan or option in plan.  For example, coverage for the 1 X salary option provided by a group term life insurance plan is calculated by multiplying the participant's plan year annualized salary by 1, with rounding up to the nearest whole 1000's of dollars.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_CVRD_DPNT_CTFN_PRVDD
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_CVRD_DPNT_CTFN_PRVDD  identifies the type of certification that  was provided by a participant for a dependent covered by one of his or her  benefits.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_DED_SCHED_PY_FREQ
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_DED_SCHED_PY_FREQ  correlates a payroll cycle to an activity base rate's  deduction  schedule.  This information is used to determine when comp deductions are  scheduled to be processed for persons assigned to a payroll  cycle.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_DOCS_PRVDD_DTLS
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_DOCS_PRVDD_DTLS stores the details of the Document uploaded against certification requirements along with the validity details

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_DOC_IMAGES
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_DOC_IMAGES for documents and images

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_DPNT_CVG_RQD_RLSHP
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_DPNT_CVG_RQD_RLSHP_F identifies the relationship between a participant and a dependent which must be satisfied in order for a  participant to designate a dependent for a program, plan type in program,   or plan.  These requirements are associated with a dependent  coverage eligibility profile and not directly to the program, plan in program or plan.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_DPNT_CVRD_ANTHR_OIPL_CVG_F
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_DPNT_CVRD_ANTHR_OIPL_CVG_F  identifies under which other options in plans a person must have or have had coverage in order to be eligible for coverage under this dependent eligbility profile(most common)or options in plans under which the person may not have or have had coverage in or to  qualify (less common). . BEN_DPNT_CVRD_ANTHR_OIPL_CVG_F  is the  intersection of BEN_DPNT_CVG_ELIGY_PRFL_F  and BEN_OIPL_F.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_DPNT_CVRD_ANTHR_PL_CVG_F
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_DPNT_CVRD_ANTHR_PL_CVG_F identifies under which other plans a person must have or have had coverage in order to be eligible for coverage under this dependent eligbility profile (most common) or plans under which the person may not have or have had coverage in or to qualify (less common). . BEN_DPNT_CVRD_ANTHR_PL_CVG_F is the intersection of BEN_DPNT_CVG_ELIGY_PRFL_F and   BEN_PL_F

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_DSGNTR_ENRLD_CVG
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_DSGNTR_ENRLD_CVG_F  identifies whether the participant, must be  enrolled in the plan or option in order for the designated dependent to be eligible for coverage.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_DSGN_RQMT_F
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_DSGN_RQMT_F  identifies for a plan, option in plan, or  option, any designation restrictions based on relationship to participant and/or number of dependents or beneficiaries designatable.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_DSGN_RQMT_RLSHP_TYP
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_DSGN_RQMT_RLSHP_TYP identifies for a desigation  requirement and plan, option in plan, or option, any designation  restrictions based on the designees' relationship to the participant.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_ELCN_RULES
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_ELCN_RULES identifies the enrollment dependency rules. A rule enforces the person to enroll into dependent opportunities. For example, person must enroll into Employee only option in HDHP plan in order to enroll into Employee only option in HSA plan.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_ELCN_RULES_TL
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_ELCN_RULES identifies the enrollment dependency rules. A rule enforces the person to enroll into dependent opportunities. For example, person must enroll into Employee only option in HDHP plan in order to enroll into Employee only option in HSA plan.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_ELCTBL_CHC_CTFN
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_ELCTBL_CHC_CTFN  identifies certifications that may be  required if a person elects to enroll in a plan, option or benefit.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_ELIGY_CRITERIA
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: To hold the information about the new criteria defined by user (with a criteria_type = 'USER'), what is the valid list of values for the criteria and the table.column, which should be retrieved for a person to be validated against the criteria values

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_ELIGY_CRIT_VALUES
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_ELIGY_CRIT_VALUES stores eligibility criteria values for user defined criteria.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_ELIGY_PRFL
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_ELIGY_PRFL_F  identifies one or more fixed eligibility factors or rules which collectively define a profile of eligibility which must be satisfied in order for a person to be found eligible for a compensation  object. Profiles are provided so that those compensation objects  which have the same eligibility requirements may share one definition, as opposed to requiring the user to maintain redundant definitions. Eligibility profile criteria are compared to the criteria for a person to  determine whether the person is eligible to participate in a compensation object.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_ELIGY_PRFL_RL
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_ELIGY_PRFL_RL_F specifies the rules that may apply to the  profile set up for determining eligibility to participate in a plan or program.  . An example is that a person must have been rehired within one year of his or her termination date.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_ELIG_AGE_PRTE
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_ELIG_AGE_PRTE_F  identifies which age factors are included in (most typical usage) or excluded from (least typical) an eligibility  profile. These age-related requirements must be satisfied in order for the person to be eligible to participate in the compensation  object. . BEN_ELIG_AGE_PRTE_F  is the intersection of BEN_ELIGY_PRFL_F  and BEN_AGE_FCTR.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_ELIG_ANTHR_PL_PRTE
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_ELIG_ANTHR_PL_PRTE_F identifies which plans are included (most typical usage) or excluded from (least typical) an eligibility profile. These criteria must be satisfied in order for the person to be eligible to participate in the compensation object. BEN_ELIG_ANTHR_PL_PRTE_F is the intersection of BEN_ELIGY_PRFL_F and BEN_PL_F.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_ELIG_ASNT_SET_PRTE
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_ELIG_ASNT_SET_PRTE_F  identifies which assignment sets are included in (most typical usage) or excluded from (least typical) an eligibility profile. These requirements must be satisfied in order for the person to be eligible to participate in the compensation  object. . BEN_ELIG_ASNT_SET_PRTE_F is the intersection of BEN_ELIGY_PRFL_F and BEN_ASNT_SET.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_ELIG_BENFTS_GRP_PRTE
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_ELIG_BENFTS_GRP_PRTE_F  identifies which benefits groups are included in (most typical usage) or excluded from (least typical) an eligibility profile.  These criteria must be satisfied in order for the person to be eligible to participate in the compensation object. . BEN_ELIG_BENFTS_GRP_PRTE_F is the intersection of BEN_ELIGY_PRFL_F and BEN_BENFTS_GRP.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_ELIG_BRGNG_UNIT_PRTE
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_ELIG_BRGNG_UNIT_PRTE_F  identifies which bargaining units are included in (most typical usage) or excluded from (least typical) an eligibility profile.  These criteria must be satisfied in order for the person to be eligible to participate in the compensation object.  BEN_ELIG_BRGNG_UNIT_PRTE_F is the intersection of BEN_ELIGY_PRFL_F and HR_ALL_ORGANIZATION_UNITS.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_ELIG_BU_PRTE
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_ELIG_BU_PRTE  identifies which business units are included in (most typical usage) or excluded from (least typical) an eligibility  profile. These bnusiness unit-related requirements must be satisfied in order for the person to be eligible to participate in the compensation  object.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_ELIG_CATEGORY
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_ELIG_CATEGORY identifies various criteria categories within eligibility profiles.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_ELIG_CBR_QUALD_BNF
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_ELIG_CBR_QUALD_BNF_F  identifies that COBRA  qualified beneficiaries are included in (most typical usage) or excluded from (least typical) an eligibility profile.  These criteria must be satisfied in order for the person to be eligible to participate in the compensation  object. . BEN_ELIG_CBR_QUALD_BNF_F is the intersection of BEN_ELIGY_PRFL and BEN_CBR_QUALD_BNF.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_ELIG_CMBN_AGE_LOS_PRTE
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_ELIG_CMBN_AGE_LOS_PRTE_F  identifies that a combination of age and length of service are included in (most typical usage) or excluded from (least typical) an eligibility profile.  These criteria must be satisfied in order for the person to be eligible to participate in the compensation object.   BEN_ELIG_CMBN_AGE_LOS_PRTE_F  is the intersection of BEN_ELIGY_PRFL  and BEN_CMBN_AGE_LOS_FCTR.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_ELIG_COMPTNCY_PRTE
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_ELIG_COMPTNCY_PRTE_F identifies which competencies are included (most typical usage) or excluded from (least typical) an eligibility profile. These criteria must be satisfied in order for the person to be eligible to participate in the compensation object. BEN_ELIG_COMPTNCY_PRTE_F is the intersection of BEN_ELIGY_PRFL_F and PER_COMPETENCES.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_ELIG_COMP_LVL_PRTE
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_ELIG_COMP_LVL_PRTE_F specifies the minimum or maximum  compensation amount, or the minimum and maximum range of compensation required to be eligible or excluded from eligbility to participate in a program or plan.   Note that the currency used for determining person's compensation must be the same as the program or plan. . BEN_ELIG_COMP_LVL_PRTE_F  is the intersection of BEN_ELIGY_PRFL_F  and BEN_COMP_LVL_FCTR.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_ELIG_CVRD_DPNT
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_ELIG_CVRD_DPNT identifies those persons designated as dependents by a participant in the context of a program, plan type in program, or plan; the level at which dependent designations occurs is defined at the program level for plans in a program, or at the plan level for plans not in a program.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_ELIG_DPNT
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_ELIG_DPNT identifies the eligibility status of a person's contacts who have the personal relationship flag selected.  This information is updated when an electable choice is created for a person or when the determine dependent eligibility process is run.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_ELIG_DPNT_CVRD_OTHR_OIPL
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_ELIG_DPNT_CVRD_OTHR_OIPL_F  identifies which options in plans a person must be covered by or (most typical usage) or not covered by (least typical) in order to be eligible to participate in a compensation object. https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_ELIG_PER_F
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_ELIG_PER_F captures information about persons found eligible, and in some cases ineligible, for a program or plan.  Typically, being eligible to participate means that the person can make an election or be enrolled, however, there are some circumstances where enrollment is not appropriate for a plan  and being eligible does not mean that the participant can enroll at the same time as when they are eligible.  The broadest definition of participation eligibility is that the person satisfies the plan defined criteria for receiving benefits from the plan at some time.
