https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_ELIG_SCRE_WTG_F
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: Stores computed score for each eligibility criteria

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_ELIG_SP_CLNG_PRG_PRTE
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_ELIG_SP_CLNG_PRG_PRTE stores special ceiling criteria.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_ELIG_STDNT_STAT_CVG
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_ELIG_STDNT_STAT_CVG_F identifies the applicable student status which must be satisfied in order for a contact with the personal relationship flag on to be eligible for a participant to designate him or her for coverage.  These requirements are associated with a dependent coverage eligibility profile and not directly to the program, plan type in program, or plan.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_ELIG_SUB_CATEGORY
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_ELIG_SUB_CATEGORY identifies various sub categories within eligibility categories.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_ELIG_SUPPL_ROLE_PRTE
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description:
BEN_ELIG_SUPPL_ROLE_PRTE
BEN_ELIG_SUPPL_ROLE_PRTE
BEN_ELIG_SUPPL_ROLE_PRTE

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_ELIG_SVC_AREA_PRTE
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_ELIG_SVC_AREA_PRTE_F identifies a group of postal codes.  A person must live, in one of the postal codes in the service area in order to be eligible for or (most typical usage) or not eligible to participate in (least typical) a compensation object. BEN_ELIG_SVC_AREA_PRTE_F  is the intersection of BEN_ELIGY_PRFL_F  and BEN_SVC_AREA.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_ELIG_TBCO_USE_PRTE
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_ELIG_TBCO_USE_PRTE_F identifies the tobacco usages that are included (most typical usage) or excluded from (least typical) an eligibility profile. These criteria must be satisfied in order for the person to be eligible to participate in the compensation object.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_ELIG_TO_PRTE_RSN_F
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_ELIG_TO_PRTE_RSN_F identifies the impact of a life event reason on eligibility for a program, plan type in program,plan in program, plan, or option in plan.  A life event reason may be identified as causing immediate eligibility or ineligibility without checking any applicable eligibility profiles.  Examples of life events that may make a person immediately ineligible are termination of employment and death.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_ELIG_TTL_CVG_VOL_PRTE
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_ELIG_TTL_CVG_VOL_PRTE_F identifies the total coverages that are included (most typical usage) or excluded from (least typical) an eligibility profile. These criteria must be satisfied in order for the person to be eligible to participate in the compensation object.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_ELIG_TTL_PRTT_PRTE
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_ELIG_TTL_PRTT_PRTE_F identifies the total participation that is included (most typical usage) or excluded from (least typical) an eligibility profile. These criteria must be satisfied in order for the person to be eligible to participate in the compensation object.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_ELIG_WK_LOC_PRTE
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_ELIG_WK_LOC_PRTE_F identifies the work location using the value from a person's assignment to determine whether a person is eligible or in some cases is to be excluded from eligibility to participate in a compensation object. . BEN_ELIG_WK_LOC_PRTE_F  is the intersection of BEN_ELIGY_PRFL_F and HR_LOCATIONS_ALL.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_ENRT_BNFT
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_ENRT_BNFT  identifies the coverage or benefit provided by the electable choices available to an eligible person as a result of a life event or for open enrollment.  This is denormalized data, made available primarily to avoid the need for reporting and enrollment processes to calculate these values on the fly.   For example,  if a specific person chooses group term life insurance at 2 time salary, the coverage provided will be $85,000.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_ENRT_CTFN_F
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_ENRT_CTFN_F  identifies the certification which may be required in order for any person to substantiate their enrollment in a plan as a result of a life event reason. Life events include open and administrative enrollments as well as actual participant life events.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_ENRT_ENRLD_ANTHR_OIPL_F
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_ENRT_ENRLD_ANTHR_OIPL_F identifies in which options in plans a person  must or will be enrolled in if he or she elects to enroll in the option in plan associated.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_ENRT_ENRLD_ANTHR_PL_F
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_ENRT_ENRLD_ANTHR_PL_F identifies in which plans a person must or will be enrolled in if he or she elects to enroll in the plan associated.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_ENRT_PERD
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_ENRT_PERD  identifies a specific instance of an enrollment period for a program or plan for a defined type of enrollment type cycle.  For example,  the Vision flex program open enrollment for plan year 2001.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_ENRT_PERD_FOR_PL_F
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_ENRT_PERD_FOR_PL_F  identifies the plans in program for which the enrollment period has been defined.  Enrollment periods may be specified for programs or plans not in programs.  When the enrollment period is defined for a program, the user is allowed to specify to which plans in the program the enrollment period applies.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_ENRT_RT
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_ENRT_RT identifies the rates calculated for a person for a plan or option in plan.  It also identifies the values of flexible credits and imputed income values.  This is denormalized data, made available primarily to avoid the need for reporting and enrollment processes to calculate these values on the fly.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_ENTITLEMENT
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_ENTITLEMENT defines main absence entitlements.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_ENT_ABS_ATTENDNS_TYPS
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: Intersection table for BEN_ENTITLEMENT and PER_ABSENCE_ATTENDANCE_TYPES

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_EXTRACT_DETAILS_ARCH
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: This table holds the archived data from BEN_EXTRACT_REQ_DETAILS table.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_EXTRACT_ELEMENTS
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: This tbale holds basic data strcuture elements for the extract process for benefix.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_EXTRACT_FILE_DETAILS
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_EXTRACT_FILE_DETAILS  Stores enrollment XML file data.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_EXTRACT_LAYOUT
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: This tbale records the basic layout definitions for the benefit extract.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_EXTRACT_MAPPING
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: This table holds the mapping values corresponding to the existing lookup values for extract purposes

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_EXTRACT_REQUEST
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: This table holds information for each run of the extract  process for a given carrier.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_EXTRACT_REQUEST_ARCH
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: This table holds archive data from the table BEN_EXTRACT_REQUEST, for historical purposes. Periodically the data will be archived based on customer setup.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_EXTRACT_REQ_DETAILS
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: This table holds the extracted data from the extract process. The details of the person(s), plans and various other person data is extracted during ectract process and is stored in this table to be transmited to the plan carrier.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_EXTRA_INPUT_VALUES
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_EXTRA_INPUT_VALUES indentifies which extra input values are used by a standard rate.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_FF_EVAL
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_FF_EVAL contains statistical data about each formula that was run and its status.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_FF_EVAL_RSLT
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_FF_EVAL_RSLT contains statistical context name and its value or result name and its value of each formula that was run.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_FLEX_CRDT_LDGR
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: This table will hold the Flex Credit Ledger Entries.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_GAP_ABSENCE_PLANS
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_GAP_ABSENCE_PLANS table is used to store generic Absence Plans

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_GAP_DAILY_ABSENCES
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: This table is used to store daily absence details like absence date, work pattern and the level of entitlement for an absence plan.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_GAP_DURATION_SUMMARY
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description:
This table is used to store absence details like Absence start date , Absence end date, No. of days in each band level of absence.
This table will be populated with the above data when the person enrolls into the absence with the configuration for log duration summary set to Yes

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_GD_OR_SVC_TYP
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_GD_OR_SVC_TYP identifies the goods or services provided by the plan sponsor's benefits plans and may also be used to identify which goods or services are reimbursable for a plan.  For example, eye exams, noncosmetic plastic surgery, child care or computer purchases.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_GLOBAL_TRANSFER_DTLS
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: Used in Global Transfer process for Benefits

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_HOLIDAY_CALENDAR
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_HOLIDAY_CALENDAR is used to define holidays or excluded days for the absence calculation.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_HOLIDAY_CALENDAR_DETS
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_HOLIDAY_CALENDAR_DETS stores dates as the holidays or excluded days.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_HRS_WKD_IN_PERD_FCTR
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_HRS_WKD_IN_PERD_FCTR  identifies how to calculate a person's hours worked in a period for such purposes as determining eligibility and calculating rates, in those cases when hours worked in a period is a factor.  In addition, it identifies a range of hours worked, again for eligibility and rate purposes.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_INTEGRATION_KEY_MAP
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description:
BEN_INTEGRATION_KEY_MAP stores 
the alternate Key Map definitions for benefits objects.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_LEE_RSN_F
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_LEE_RSN_F identifies the parameters which control program or plan enrollment activities resulting from a specific life event reason occurring; in the context it is used here, life event includes those resulting from participant life events.  Open and administrative enrollments information is identified on enrollment period. BEN_LEE_RSN_F defines when key enrollment activities occur and how enrollment start and end dates are determined.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_LEE_RSN_RL_F
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_LEE_RSN_RL_F specifies the rules to be enforced  for a particular type of enrollment,  for a specific  reason, for a particular comensation program or plan.  It identifies a rule used to determine life event enrollment requirements in those cases when the standard, delivered method does not meet the business requirement. For example, where the life event enrollment parameters vary by location, organization unit.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_LEGAL_DISCLAIMER
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: This table will be used to hold legal disclaimers texts.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_LEG_ENTITY_DEFAULTS
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_LEG_ENTITY_DEFAULTS stores legal entity default setup for default and unrestricted relationships.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_LER_BNFT_RSTRN_CTFN_F
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_LER_BNFT_RSTRN_CTFN_F identifies which certifications may be required when a person elects to enroll in a benefit in a plan or option in plan where the enrollment opportunity is the result of a specific life event.  . For example, a person is enrolled in three times pay life insurance.  The person gets married and elects ten time pay life insurance and the plan requires proof of good health when a person jumps more than two levels.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_LER_BNFT_RSTRN_F
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_LER_BNFT_RSTRN_F identifies restrictions which may be imposed a person elects to enroll in a benefit in a plan or option in plan where the enrollment opportunity is the result of a specific life event. . For example, the plan may only allow a person to change up to two levels of life insurance without certification and up to five levels with proof of good health.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_LER_CHG_DPNT_CVG_CTFN_F
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_LER_CHG_DPNT_CVG_CTFN_F identifies the certifications that may be required as a result of a life event that allows a participant to add or remove dependents.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_LER_CHG_DPNT_CVG_F
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_LER_CHG_DPNT_CVG_F identifies the valid life events that allow a participant to add or remove dependents.Dependent eligibility profiles are then associated with the appropriate program, plan type in program, or plan.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_LER_CHG_OIPL_ENRT_F
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_LER_CHG_OIPL_ENRT_F identifies the impact of a life event reason on the election changes a person may or may not make for an option in a plan.  Each record specifies whether the parameters defined apply to persons who are currently participating in the option in plan or if, as a result of the life event, they are newly eligible to enroll.  . For example, if a person is newly eligible to enroll in the Acme medical plan because of a change of residence postal code life event, they can choose the following options  employee only, employee plus spouse and family.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_LER_CHG_OIPL_ENRT_RL_F
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_LER_CHG_OIPL_ENRT_RL_F identifies a rule  to be used to determine the parameters which control enrollment for this life event reason and option in plan.  A rule is used in those cases when the delivered, standard approach attributed in life event reason to change option in plan enrollment does not support the business requirement.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_LER_CHG_PGM_ENRT_F
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_LER_CHG_PGM_ENRT_F identifies the impact of a life event reason on the election changes a person may or may not make to a program.  Each record specifies whether the parameters defined apply to persons who are currently participating in plans or options in plan in the program or if, as a result of the life event, they are newly eligible to enroll.   . For example, a person is newly eligible to enroll in the plans in the Acme Benefits Program  because of a change of residence postal code life event.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_LER_CHG_PLIP_ENRT_F
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_LER_CHG_PLIP_ENRT_F identifies the impact of a life event reason on the election changes a person may or may not make to a plan in a program.  Each record specifies whether the parameters defined apply to persons who are currently participating in the plan in program or if, as a result of the life event, they are newly eligible to enroll.  .  For example, a person is newly eligible to enroll in the Acme Medical Plan because of a change of residence postal code life event.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_LER_CHG_PLIP_ENRT_RL_F
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_LER_CHG_PLIP_ENRT_RL_F  identifies a rule  to be used to determine the parameters which control enrollment for this life event reason and plan.  A rule is used in those cases when the delivered, standard approach attributed in life event reason to change plan enrollment  does not support the business requirement

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_LER_CHG_PL_NIP_ENRT_F
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_LER_CHG_PL_NIP_ENRT_F specifies the life events that allow an eligible person to add, change or end enrollment in a plan that is not part of a program.  . For example, if a person moves, he or she may become ineligible for his or her current medical plan, and therefore may enroll in a medical plan for which he or she has now become eligible.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_LER_CHG_PL_NIP_RL_F
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_LER_CHG_PL_NIP_RL_F identifies a rule to be used to determine the parameters which control enrollment for this life event reason and plan which is not part of a program.  A rule is used in those cases when the delivered, standard approach attributed in life event reason to change plan enrollment  does not support the business requirement

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_LER_CHG_PTIP_ENRT_F
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_LER_CHG_PTIP_ENRT_F identifies the impact of a life event reason on the election changes a person may or may not make to plans of a specified plan type in a program.  Each record specifies whether the parameters defined apply to persons who are currently participating in plans of the specified plan types in the program or if, as a result of the life event, they are newly eligible to enroll.  For example,  a person is newly eligible to enroll in  medical coverage in the Acme Benefits Program because of a change of residence postal code life event.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_LER_ENRT_CTFN_F
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_LER_ENRT_CTFN_F identifies the types of certifications that may be required  in order to enroll in a plan as a result of a specified life event.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_LER_F
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_LER_F identifies  all of the possible life event reasons that may cause a change to a person's eligibility, electabiliy, coverage, rates or premiums.   For example,  birth of a child, adoption of a child, marriage, reached maximum age to be in plan, new hire, rehire, divorced, death, spouse lost coverage, promotion, salary increase, age change.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_LER_F_TL
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_LER_F_TL holds MLS translated data from main table, BEN_LER_F..

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_LER_PER_INFO_CS_LER
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_LER_PER_INFO_CS_LER_F associates a life event reason to the discrete data changes applied for a person which collectively indicate how to detect the life event.  The database table and columns which change are defined in BEN_PER_INFO_CHG_CS_LER_F.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_LER_POPL_ACTN_TYP_F
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_LER_POPL_ACTN_TYP_F identifies the length of time a person will have to satisfy the requirements of an action type and may be defined for program or plans in programs or plans not in programs against a life event.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_LER_RLTD_PER_CS_LER
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_LER_RLTD_PER_CS_LER_F  identifies what life event reasons, when they occur for one person, signal the need to generate another life event for a person related to that individual.  . For example, the life event reason of death of a participant means that persons related to that person may have a life event reason of death of participant which provided coverage.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_LER_RL_F
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_LER_RL_F identifies one or more eligibility, document approval, and alert related rules to a life event for the purpose of determining a person's eligibility to participate, document approval setup for self reporting life events, and the alert setup respectively

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_LER_RL_F_TL
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_LER_RL_F identifies one or more eligibility, document approval, and alert related rules to a life event for the purpose of determining a person's eligibility to participate, document approval setup for self reporting life events, and the alert setup respectively

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_LE_CLSN_N_RSTR
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_LE_CLSN_N_RSTR  is a processing table and contains the information about persons and life events who have been backed out due to an overriding/winning life eventt, where the backed out information is not maintainable on the originating table.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_LE_RESOLUTIONS_F
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_LE_RESOLUTIONS_F identifies the legal entities that are striped against life event reasons or programs or plans.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_LOS_FCTR
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_LOS_FCTR  identifies how to calculate a person's length of service for such purposes as determining eligibility and calculating rates, in those cases when length of service is a factor. In  addition, it identifies a range of lengths of service, again for eligibility and rate purposes.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_OIPLIP_F
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_OIPLIP_F  exists solely for the definition of activity rates for an option in a plan in a program.  These rates supersede any rates defined for an option in a plan.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_OIPL_F
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_OIPL_F identifies the processing characteristics of an option that is provided by a plan.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_OIPL_F_TL
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_OIPL_F_TL holds MLS translated data from main table, BEN_OIPL_F.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_ONLINE_WARNINGS
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_ONLINE_WARNINGS temporarily stores warning messages written from API's.  The enrollment forms look in that table to see if any messages were written.  If so, it displays the warnings and then deletes the rows from the table.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_OPTIP_F
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_OPTIP_F identifies the processing requirements for an option in a plan type in a program . BEN_OPTIP_F is  used for flex credit rates.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_OPT_F
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_OPT_F  identifies compensation alternatives, typically in coverage and investment choices,  which are provided by one or more plans.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_OPT_F_TL
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_OPT_F_TL holds MLS translated data from main table, BEN_OPT_F..

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_PCT_FL_TM_FCTR
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_PCT_FL_TM_FCTR  identifies a range of percent full time values for such purposes as determining eligibility and calculating rates, in those cases when percent full time is a factor.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_PERSON_ACTIONS
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description:
BEN_PERSON_ACTIONS identifies persons
                                         being processed in a benefits
                                         concurrent manager process.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_PER_ACTY_LOG
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_PER_ACTY_LOG contains information related to person activity on self service enrollment page.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_PER_BENEFIT_GROUP_F
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: This table is to store the employees benefit Group information.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_PER_BILL_INFO_F
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_PER_BILL_INFO_F contains information related to Benefits Billing Information and it stores common billing info for person.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_PER_BILL_INFO_F_
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_PER_BILL_INFO_F contains information related to Benefits Billing Information and it stores common billing info for person.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_PER_BNFTS_BAL_F
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_PER_BNFTS_BAL_F  identifies the value of a benefits balance for a person.  For example, you may record the previous 30 years of benefits base pay for each participant.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_PER_BNFTS_BAL_F_
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_PER_BNFTS_BAL_F  identifies the value of a benefits balance for a person.  For example, you may record the previous 30 years of benefits base pay for each participant.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_PER_BNF_ORG
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_PER_BNF_ORG identifies the organization's or trustee's that can be designated by a participant as the beneficiary of plan benefits. For e.g., a person may want an NGO as beneficiary of his or her life insurance benefits.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_PER_DLVRY_MTHD_F
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_PER_DLVRY_MTHD_F  identifies the communication delivery method preferred by a person.  For example, George Green prefers to have communications delivered via email.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_PER_ELIG_RSLT_DTLS_GT
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: ben_per_elig_rslt_track_dtls holds the eligibility criteria evaluation results for each criteria of the eligibility profile and or the eligibility objects

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_PER_ELIG_RSLT_GT
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: ben_per_elig_rslt_track table holds data to track top level row for eligibility profile id or eligibility obj id

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_PER_INFO_CHG_CS_LER
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_PER_INFO_CHG_CS_LER_F identifies a specific change to a column in a table that, when combined with others, make up a profile from which life events can be detected as person's data change.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_PER_IN_LER
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_PER_IN_LER identifies all life events for a person, including identifying which life event, if any, is currently in progress for the specified person.  This table differs from BEN_PTNL_LER_FOR_PER  in that it identifies persons whose detected life events  have been scrubbed by the manage life event processes and classified as legitimate or, through user input, have been created.  It should be noted that persons in open and administrative enrollments are also maintained on this table.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_PER_IN_LER_
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_PER_IN_LER identifies all life events for a person, including identifying which life event, if any, is currently in progress for the specified person.  This table differs from BEN_PTNL_LER_FOR_PER  in that it identifies persons whose detected life events  have been scrubbed by the manage life event processes and classified as legitimate or, through user input, have been created.  It should be noted that persons in open and administrative enrollments are also maintained on this table.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_PER_LE_HABITS_COV_F
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: This table to track employees smoking habbits.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_PER_LE_HABITS_COV_F_
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: This table to track employees smoking habbits.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_PGM_DPNT_CVG_CTFN_F
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_PGM_DPNT_CVG_CTFN_F  specifies the certifications  that may be required when persons designate dependents under the terms of the program. . For example, a participant requests the addition of a new dependent  for medical coverage in the ABC Benefits Program.  He or she may be required to provide proof of birth or adoption.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_PGM_F
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description:
BEN_PGM_F identifies the processing characteristics for a compensation program.  A program is a way to group plans together which are typically offered as a package of benefits to a group of people.  Attributes of the program which control  participation eligibility are logically inherited down to the plans which are defined to it,  this allows plan sponsors to establish plan definitions more easily and is a primary means of determining what plans should be put into a program.  Additionally, this table defines the standard unit of measure and reference period  for activity rates used by  the plans in the program.  
For example, Acme Flex Program, Acme Assigned Benefits for New Hires, Acme COBRA Program, Acme Executive Benefits Program.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_PGM_F_TL
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_PGM_F_TL holds MLS translated data from main table, BEN_PGM_F..

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_PIL_ELCTBL_CHC_POPL
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_PIL_ELCTBL_CHC_POPL identifies the persons and the programs or plans not in program for which they have electable choices as a result of a life event.  It contains information such as  the enrollment period start and end dates, default assigned date and theelections made date.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_PLIP_F
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_PLIP_F identifies and specifies processing characteristics of a plan assigned to a program.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_PL_BNF
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_PL_BNF is the person or organization designated by a participant in a plan as the beneficiary of plan benefits.  . For e.g.,  a person may designate his or her spouse as beneficiary of his or her life insurance benefits.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_PL_BNF_
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_PL_BNF is the person or organization designated by a participant in a plan as the beneficiary of plan benefits.  . For e.g.,  a person may designate his or her spouse as beneficiary of his or her life insurance benefits.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_PL_BNF_CTFN_F
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_PL_BNF_CTFN_F identifies the certfication that may be required of a participant designating beneficiaries for a plan.  . For example,  a person may be required to provide a notarized statement of non-spousal designation when designating a beneficiary other than his or her spouse.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_PL_BNF_CTFN_PRVDD
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_PL_BNF_CTFN_PRVDD  identifies the certfications  that have been provided by a participant designating beneficiaries for a plan.   . For example,  a person may have provided a notarized statement of non-spousal designation as a result of designating a beneficiary other than his or her spouse.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_PL_CARRIER
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: This table holds information of  the benefit plan carrier, and the information pertaining to how the carrier expects the extracted data to be sent.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_PL_COPY_EXIST_OBJ
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: This table stores the details of existing objects that can be reused while exporting or importing a plan or program.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_PL_COPY_LOGS
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: This table stores the log data created while exporting or importing the plan or program.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_PL_COPY_MAP
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: This table stores the list of objects that require mapping while exporting or importing a plan.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_PL_COPY_PARAMS
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: This table stores parameters required for exporting or importing a plan or program.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_PL_DPNT_CVG_CTFN_F
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_PL_DPNT_CVG_CTFN_F identifies the certification that may be required in order to designate a person as a dependent when dependent designation is defined at the plan level.  . For example, a person may be required to provided proof of birth or adoption when adding a child for the Stay Healthy Medical Plan coverage.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_PL_EXTRACT_IDENTIFIER_F
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: The ANSI 834 extract delivered by Oracle allows customers to send their enrollment changes to their Plan providers. Some plan providers allocate different plan identifiers for the same plans and options depending on the indicative data of the employee who is enrolled. This table identifies multiple identifiers for the same plan or option in plan, based on the Indicative data stored on their employee assignment and address.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_PL_F
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_PL_F identifies a discrete and formally defined compensation offering which is categorized by only one plan type, has one or more activity base rates, shares a known set of eligibility criteria and when applicable, has a fixed set of provided option. For health and welfare benefits, a plan is provided by a  third-party administrator or carrier.   Plan has a number of columns which are prefixed with "NIP," indicating not in program, such columns are used only when the plan is not incorporated into a program,all the remaining attributes apply to the characteristics of the plan either independent of any program affinity or in the context of the program. Examples are  Acme Be Well HMO,Acme Stock Purchase Plan, Acme HQ Employee Savings Plan.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_PL_F_TL
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_PL_F_TL holds MLS translated data from main table, BEN_PL_F..

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_PL_GD_OR_SVC_F
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_PL_GD_OR_SVC_F  contains the list of goods or services that may be reimbursed by the compensation plan.  A process will validate whether or not these goods or services may be reimbursed as taxable or nontaxable.  Generally, reimbursements are nontaxable.  BEN_PL_GD_OR_SVC_F may also identify  the goods or services provided by a plan.  For example, the Medical FSA reimburses deductible amounts and prescription drugs.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_PL_GD_R_SVC_CTFN_F
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_PL_GD_R_SVC_CTFN_F identifies what certification may be required to substantiate a reimbursement claim  for the plan.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_PL_PCP
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_PL_PCP identifies all  care provider  limitations specific to each plan.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_PL_PCP_TYP
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_PL_PCP_TYP  identifies all  care provider limitations specific to each plan and care provider type.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_PL_REGN_F
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_PL_REGN_F is an identifiable section or part of legislation, rule, code, or policy pertinent to the proper management of this plan or reporting group.  In addition to correlating a regulation to a plan or reporting group, plan regulation provides a means for the plan sponsor to identify rules for determining participants who are tracked in the  United States for benefits nondiscrimination testing.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_PL_REGY_BOD_F
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_PL_REGY_BOD_F identifies a legislative or policy making agency which dictates or enforces rules for this plan or reporting group.  In the United States, for example, the Internal Revenue Service  regulates most health and welfare plans.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_PL_REGY_PRP_F
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_PL_REGY_PRP_F identifies the role and purpose for this regulatory body in the administration of this plan.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_PL_TYP_EXTEND_PERIOD
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_PL_TYP_EXTEND_PERIOD identifies the benefit plan types to extend the assessment period.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_PL_TYP_F
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_PL_TYP_F categorizes plans according to the type of compensation benefit provided.  A plan can have only one plan type associated with it.  For example, Flex, Medical, Dental, Vision, Employee Group Life Insurance, Disability, Savings, Health Care Flexible Spending Account or dependent Care Flexible Spending Account.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_PL_TYP_F_TL
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_PL_TYP_F_TL holds MLS translated data from main table, BEN_PL_TYP_F..

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_PL_TYP_OPT_TYP_F
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_PL_TYP_OPT_TYP_F identifies the specific plan types and the option types that are valide for each plan type.  This enables the presentation of only  those options valid for a certain plan type, for example,  medical plan types offer coverage options of employee only, employee plus one and employee plus family  life, insurance type plans offer options of 1 times pay and 2 times pay.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_POPL_ACTN_TYP_F
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_POPL_ACTN_TYP_F  identifies the length of time a person will have to satisfy the requirements of an action type and may be defined for program or plans in programs or plans not in programs.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_POPL_ENRT_TYP_CYCL
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_POPL_ENRT_TYP_CYCL keeps track of which program/plan is offered by default or unrestricted modes.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_POPL_ORG_F
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_POPL_ORG_F identifies those external or internal organizations responsible for providing goods or services to  this plan or program. For example, third party agents, self insured companies.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_POPL_ORG_ROLE_F
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_POPL_ORG_ROLE_F identifies the services or functions performed by an organization for this program or plan.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_POPL_RPTG_GRP_F
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_POPL_RPTG_GRP_F establishes affinity between a program or plan and a reporting group.  Plans and programs are grouped together for a number of reasons ,more importantly, however, the plan sponsor may have to group plans or programs in order to identify that they are, in essence, the same thing.This is necessary in those cases when for plan design purposes, the user must create multiple plan records for the same underlying plan. For example, they are reported together as a group for regulatory purposes, their financial statements are combined, the participants are reported against as a group.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_POPL_VALD_HRCHY
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_POPL_VALD_HRCHY identifies the various paths within a program or plan not in program hierarchy which are validated against the predefined set of reporting rules.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_POPL_YR_PERD
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_POPL_YR_PERD identifies the years for which this plan or program is active.  The actual plan years ,with start and stop dates, are associated to the plan or program.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_POP_UP_MESSAGES
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_POP_UP_MESSAGES identify the messages associated with various steps in specific benefits forms.  . For example, display the message "First Time Enrollee" after the enrollment form has opened for a person who has no previous enrollment results.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_PRMRY_CARE_PRVDR
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_PRMRY_CARE_PRVDR identifies the care providers, physicians, and chiropractors, for a participant enrolled in a plan or option in plan or for a dependent covered by a participant.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_PRTL_MO_RT_PRTN_VAL_F
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_PRTL_MO_RT_PRTN_VAL_F identifies how to prorate rates , in those cases when a participant's enrollment coverage date falls within a month and the plan requires that  rates be prorated based upon the date during the month the participant's coverage started.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_PRTN_ELIGY_RL_F
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_PRTN_ELIGY_RL_F  contains rules that are applied for eligibility to participate in a plan or program.   A program or plan may specify one or more BEN_PRTN_ELIGY_RL_F instead of or in addition to specifying one or more profiles.  BEN_PRTN_ELIGY_RL_F are always applied in addition to any eligibility profiles and the rules that apply to the profiles.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_PRTN_ELIG_F
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_PRTN_ELIG_F identifies participation eligibility criteria for a program, plan types in program, plan in program, plan, or option in plan.  Criteria for participation eligibility are  defined as rules ,through an association with one or more BEN_ELIGY_PRFL_F, or using both approaches.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_PRTN_ELIG_PRFL_F
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_PRTN_ELIG_PRFL_F identifies one or more eligibility profiles to a  program, plan type in program, plan in program, plan, or option in plan for the purpose of determining a person's eligibility to participate.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_PRTT_ASSOCD_INSTN_F
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_PRTT_ASSOCD_INSTN_F  identifies multiple values for reimbursement or other processing instructions.  For example, if a person requests reimbursement for a higher amount than is currently available, each increment reimbursed  of part of the requested value will be associated with each additional increment thus identifying the parts of the total payment.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_PRTT_CLM_GD_OR_SVC_TYP
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_PRTT_CLM_GD_OR_SVC_TYP identifies the provided goods and/or services for which a participant is seeking reimbursement.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_PRTT_ENRT_ACTN
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_PRTT_ENRT_ACTN identifies any outstanding enrollment action required of a person in order for elections or life events to be unsuspended or, at a minimum, considered complete.  For example, participants must provided evidence of good health if they are to enrolled in a group term life insurance policy at a high coverage volume.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_PRTT_ENRT_CTFN_PRVDD
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_PRTT_ENRT_CTFN_PRVDD identifies the certifications that have been provided by a participant in a plan in order to satisfy the enrollment certification requirements.  For example, a participant elected ten times pay life insurance coverage and provided proof of good health.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_PRTT_ENRT_RSLT
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_PRTT_ENRT_RSLT identifies the plans or option in plans in which a participant is enrolled, either through explicit election or due to system default/automatic enrollments.  It always identifies a plan and the life event reason ,which, in this context may be an open enrollment, that caused the enrollment.  Where applicable , the elected program and option are identified.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_PRTT_ENRT_RSLT_
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_PRTT_ENRT_RSLT identifies the plans or option in plans in which a participant is enrolled, either through explicit election or due to system default/automatic enrollments.  It always identifies a plan and the life event reason ,which, in this context may be an open enrollment, that caused the enrollment.  Where applicable , the elected program and option are identified.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_PRTT_LEG_DISCMR_ACTN
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_PRTT_LEG_DISCMR_ACTN contains the status of the action taken by the self-service user against a legal disclaimer. The status can be either Accept/Decline.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_PRTT_RT_VAL
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_PRTT_RT_VAL identifies the values, tax consequence and other information about standard rates, total flex credits and imputed income calculated for a person enrolled in a plan or option in plan.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_PRTT_RT_VAL_CTFN_PRVDD
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_PRTT_RT_VAL_CTFN_PRVDD identifies the certifications that have been provided for a rate.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_PSTL_ZIP_RNG
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: Range of postal codes. You create postal zip ranges by providing start and end zip codes.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_PTIP_DPNT_CVG_CTFN_F
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_PTIP_DPNT_CVG_CTFN_F  identifies the certifications that may be required when participants designate contacts that have been determine to be eligible for coverage under the terms identifies by the plan type  for the plan or option in plan under which the participant is covering the dependent.  .  For example,  a participant requests the addition of a new dependent for the Stay Healthy Family medical coverage and a certification of proof of birth or adoption is required for all Medical Plans in the program.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_PTIP_F
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_PTIP_F  identifies processing requirements for a plan type that is supported within a program.  For example, the ABC Corporation Flexible Benefits Program is composed of Medical, Dental, Vision, Employee Group Term Life Insurance, Health Care Flexible Spending Account, and Dependent Care Flexible Spending Account types of plans.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_PTNL_LER_FOR_PER
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_PTNL_LER_FOR_PER for a person is an occurrence of data change detection in person indicative data for a person, an event that is detected as a result of the examination of the passage of time, or a scheduled event that is explicitly assigned to the person.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_PTNL_LER_FOR_PER_
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_PTNL_LER_FOR_PER for a person is an occurrence of data change detection in person indicative data for a person, an event that is detected as a result of the examination of the passage of time, or a scheduled event that is explicitly assigned to the person.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_PYMT_SCHED_PY_FREQ
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_PYMT_SCHED_PY_FREQ  correlates a payroll cycle to an activity base rate's payment  schedule.  This information is used to determine when compensation payments are scheduled to be processed for persons assigned to a pay cycle

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_QSTNR_RESPONSES
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_QSTNR_RESPONSES contains responses submitted for the benefits questionnaires.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_REGN_F
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_REGN_F identifies a discreet rule, policy, or requirement defined by a governmental or policy making body  which may govern the administration of one or more benefits.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_REGN_FOR_REGY_BODY_F
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_REGN_FOR_REGY_BODY_F identifies the discreet piece of legislation or policy and the group responsible for creating or administering it.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_REGN_F_TL
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_REGN_F_TL holds MLS translated data from main table, BEN_REGN_F..

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_SCHEDD_ENRT_RL_F specifies the rules to be enforced  for a particular type of scheduled enrollment period and  for a particular compensation program or plan. For example , where the scheduled enrollment parameters vary by location, organization unit.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_VRBL_MTCHG_RT_F
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_VRBL_MTCHG_RT_F identifies a rate or rates  the plan sponsor adds to a participant's activity rate, based upon the activity rate the participant elects, where the amount the plan sponsor contributes varies by some factor, such as compensation or a rule that determines whether the person is highly compensated.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_VRBL_RT_ELIG_PRFL_F
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: Intersection table of Variable rate Profile and Eligibility Profile tables.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_VRBL_RT_PRFL_F
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_VRBL_RT_PRFL_F identifies the values for a rate based on that fact that a person satisfies the criteria that have been defined for the profile.  BEN_VRBL_RT_PRFL_F are reusable and may be applied to one or more standard rates, credits, imputed income, coverage or premiums.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_VRBL_RT_PRFL_RL_F
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_VRBL_RT_PRFL_RL_F identifies a rule  that may be used to determine the criteria, such as whether a person is a key employee,which is not available through the criteria delivered for BEN_VRBL_RT_PRFL_F.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_VRBL_RT_RL_F
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_VRBL_RT_RL_F identifiesa rule  that may be used to calculate an additional amount to adjust the values determined by BEN_ACTY_BASE_RT_F.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_VSTG_AGE_RQMT
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_VSTG_AGE_RQMT identifies the minimum and maximum age ranges which are then used to determine vesting values for a rate.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_VSTG_LOS_RQMT
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_VSTG_LOS_RQMT identifies the minimum and maximum length of service  ranges which are then used to determine vesting values for a rate.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_VSTG_SCHED_F
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_VSTG_SCHED_F  identifies the progressive periods on which a person becomes vested in benefits.  Vesting schedules may be applied to benefit accruals for retirement plans, employer contributions made to savings plans, and stock option grants.  For example, stock options  are 4-year graded at 25% per year and have a  5-year cliff.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_WORK_PATTERN
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_WORK_PATTERN identifies  default work pattern.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_WORK_PATTERN_DETAILS
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_WORK_PATTERN_DETAILS identifies work pattern details.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_WTHN_YR_PERD
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_WTHN_YR_PERD identifies the various  start and end dates for periods.  . For example, quarters and half years that occur within a year period that starts February 1 and ends March 31.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_WV_PRTN_RSN_CTFN_PL_F
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_WV_PRTN_RSN_CTFN_PL_F  identifies the certification that may be required when a person waives coverage in a plan for a specified reason as opposed to actively electing a "Waive" plan.  For example, if an  eligible person waives participation in a plan for the reason of "have other coverage", he or she may be required to provide proof of coverage in an external plan.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_WV_PRTN_RSN_CTFN_PTIP_F
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_WV_PRTN_RSN_CTFN_PTIP_F identifies the certification that may be required when a person waives coverage in a plan type in program for a specified reason as opposed to actively electing a "Waive" plan.  For example, if an  eligible person waives participation in all medical plans for the reason of "have other coverage", he or she may be required to provide proof of coverage in an external plan.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_WV_PRTN_RSN_PL_F
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_WV_PRTN_RSN_PL_F  identifies the valid reasons a person may waive coverage in a plan  as opposed to actively electing a "Waive" plan.  For example, if an  eligible person waives participation in a plan, it may be for the reason of "have other coverage".

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_WV_PRTN_RSN_PTIP_F
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_WV_PRTN_RSN_PTIP_F identifies the valid reasons a person may waive coverage in a plan type as opposed to actively electing a "Waive" plan.  For example, if an  eligible person waives participation in all medical plans, it may be for the reason of "have other coverage".

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: BEN_YR_PERD
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: BEN_YR_PERD  identifies the start and stop dates for a plan year which may apply to multiple plans and/or programs.  Typically, the elapsed time represents a year; however, in certain limited situations such as, a short year necessitated by realigning the plan's year, the period of time can be less than a year.  This period of time is an important factor for many plans.   For example: some plans specify year-to-date contribution limits that apply to this year instead of to a calendar or fiscal year also  in some countries, many plans require that participant elections remain in force for the duration of the plan unless a life event occurs which allows an enrollment change.The financial disclosure of plan assets and liabilities may be stated in the plan year.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: CMP_SALARY
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description:
Salary holds details of salary proposals for Employee or Contingent worker assignments. A salary proposal may have one or more components, held in Salary Components. There can be  one/multiple unapproved salary proposal(s) at any time. When a salary proposal is approved, an element entry of type 'SP' (Salary Proposal) is created or updated to reflect the approval. The effective start date of the change to the salary element entry is the same as that of Salary Change date.

The details of Salary Components cannot be updated or inserted if the overall proposal has already been approved. A Salary may have one or multiple components associated with it. Approval of all components are handled in one transaction and cannot be addressed separately.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: CMP_SALARY_
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description:
Salary holds details of salary proposals for Employee or Contingent worker assignments. A salary proposal may have one or more components, held in Salary Components. There can be  one/multiple unapproved salary proposal(s) at any time. When a salary proposal is approved, an element entry of type 'SP' (Salary Proposal) is created or updated to reflect the approval. The effective start date of the change to the salary element entry is the same as that of Salary Change date.

The details of Salary Components cannot be updated or inserted if the overall proposal has already been approved. A Salary may have one or multiple components associated with it. Approval of all components are handled in one transaction and cannot be addressed separately.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: CMP_SALARY_BACKUP
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: Temporary salary backup table for future salary proposal during global transfer flow.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: CMP_SALARY_BASES
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: Salary Basis is the entity that establishes the period of time for which an employee's salary is quoted, typically either Monthly, Hourly or Annually. Salary Basis also allows a link to a payroll element or a link to a Grade Rate.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: CMP_SALARY_COMPONENTS
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description:
Salary holds details of salary proposals for Employee or Contingent worker assignments. A salary proposal may have one or more components, held in Salary Components. There can be  one/multiple unapproved salary proposal(s) at any time. 

The details of Salary Components cannot be updated or inserted if the overall proposal has already been approved. A Salary may have one or multiple components associated with it. Approval of all components are handled in one transaction and cannot be addressed separately.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: CMP_SALARY_COMPONENTS_
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description:
Salary holds details of salary proposals for Employee or Contingent worker assignments. A salary proposal may have one or more components, held in Salary Components. There can be  one/multiple unapproved salary proposal(s) at any time. 

The details of Salary Components cannot be updated or inserted if the overall proposal has already been approved. A Salary may have one or multiple components associated with it. Approval of all components are handled in one transaction and cannot be addressed separately.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: CMP_TCS_PER_PERD_STMT
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description:
Table that stores the statement information for a person. This is a child of cmp_tcs_per_perd. 
A record is created for a employer for a person. If a person has more than one employer data or a person has any data at person level, the process automatically create a record for  All employer (null legal_entity_id).  If a person has only one employer (one_assignment_id)  then there will not be a all employer record ( al the information will be assign to the employer)

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: CMP_TCS_STMT_PAGE_DEF
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description:
Stores the following details for the category page, summary page and summary page section: description, page title, additional information, graph and the table column details.
For item page the table column details are stored with the item_detail_flag set to Y with the cat_id as the parent category id.
For Summary page sections the cat_type_cd is set to MNTRY_SECT or NNMNTRY_SECT and SMRY to Summary page details.
For Categories the Cat_Type_cd is set to the category type and the cat_id indicates the category id.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: FF_FDI_USAGES
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: This table contains compiler-generated descriptions of database items used in a formula.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: This table contains formula definitions and text.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: This table contains translated formula names and descriptions.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: FF_FORMULA_TYPES_B
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: This table contains formula types, which define groups of formulas to associate with formula contexts and used for specific purposes.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: FF_FORMULA_TYPES_TL
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: This table contains translated formula types.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: FF_FTYPE_CONTEXT_USAGES
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: This table contains formula contexts for each formula type.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: FF_FUNCTIONS_B
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: This table contains formula function definitions.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: FF_FUNCTIONS_TL
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: This table contains translated information for FF_FUNCTIONS_B.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: FF_FUNCTION_PARAMETERS
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: This table contains the parameters for specific formula functions.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: FF_FUNC_CONTEXT_USAGES
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: This table contains the formula contexts required by a function, where a context is a unique identifier that the formula can act on, such as assignment ID.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: FF_GLOBALS_B_F
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: This table contains global values and data types.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: FF_GLOBALS_TL
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: This table contains translated global names and descriptions.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: FF_ROUTES_B
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: This table contains FROM and WHERE clauses of a SQL statement for a specific database item.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: FF_ROUTES_TL
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: This table contains translated information for FF_ROUTES.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: FF_ROUTE_CONTEXT_USAGES
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: This table contains a single usage of a context as a bind variable in the ROUTE SQLstatement.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: FF_ROUTE_PARAMETERS
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: This table contains parameters that can be passed to a ROUTE to restrict the number of rows returned by the ROUTE.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: FF_ROUTE_PARAMETER_VALS
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: This table contains values provided for route parameters by a database item.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: FF_ROUTE_TO_DESCR_FLEXS
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: Setup table for database item creation for descriptive flexfields.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: FF_RULE_LINES_F
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: This table contains values for rule lines that help to create formulas.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: FF_USER_COLUMNS
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: FF_USER_COLUMNS holds the definitions for columns in user defined tables. FORMULA_ID is the foreign key to the validation formula that should trigger when a user inserts/updates a new value for the column.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: FF_USER_COLUMNS_TL
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: Stores translations for FF_USER_COLUMNS.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: FF_USER_COLUMN_INSTANCES_F
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: FF_USER_COLUMN_INSTANCES_F is the date-tracked table that holds the actual values for each row and column combination in a specific user defined table. There is a row for each cell of the user table.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: FF_USER_ENTITIES_B
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: This table contains table and selection criteria combinations used to define database items.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: FF_USER_ENTITIES_TL
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: This table contains translated information for FF_USER_ENTITIES_B.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: FF_USER_ROWS_F
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: FF_USER_ROWS_F is a date-tracked table that holds the definitions for rows in user defined tables. Rows can hold an exact value, or a low-high range of values to provide the match to a supplied user value. For example you may want to define one table with rows based on ranges of salary values, and another table based on exact grade values.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: FF_USER_ROWS_TL
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: Stores translations for FF_USER_ROWS_F.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: FF_USER_TABLES
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: FF_USER_TABLES holds the table definitions for user defined tables. These tables are matrix structures of rows and columns that maintain date-tracked lists of values stored as cells for specific row/column combinations. Rows are defined in FF_USER_ROWS_F, columns are defined in FF_USER_COLUMNS and the actual cell values, which change over time, are held in FF_USER_COLUMN_INSTANCES_F. For example, you could define a table of union-negotiated rates for overtime and standard time that are dependent on grade level. The user table, UNION RATES, would have a row for each GRADE, and each row would be identified by an exact match with a specific grade. The table would have two columns, STANDARD RATE and OVERTIME RATE.

https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.name: FF_USER_TABLES_TL
https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/hu.description: Stores translations for FF_USER_TABLES.
